task_completion.description: Verify the agent opens or reads the EarthMapper-3 orbit requirements document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (e.g., using 'cat' or 'less') displaying the contents of /home/ga/Desktop/earthmapper3_orbit_requirements.txt.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent creates the required GMAT script file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named earthmapper3_orbit.script is visible in a text editor, IDE, or terminal file listing within the /home/ga/GMAT_output/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the GMAT script is configured to propagate the satellite for one full repeat cycle (16 days).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GMAT script contents or GMAT GUI shows a propagation stopping condition set to 16 days (e.g., 'Propagate ... {Sat.ElapsedDays = 16}').

task_completion.description: Verify the agent creates the required results text file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file named groundtrack_design_results.txt is visible in a text editor or terminal file listing within the /home/ga/GMAT_output/ directory.

task_completion.description: Verify the results file contains the required orbital parameters with values within the acceptable ranges.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of groundtrack_design_results.txt show SMA_km (~7077.7), INC_deg (~98.19), ECC (< 0.002), altitude_km, longitude_drift_deg, repeat_cycle_revolutions (233), and sun_sync_RAAN_rate_degperday.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually ran GMAT to compute the orbit, rather than hallucinating the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The GMAT GUI is open and showing the mission run, or a terminal window shows the execution of the GMAT script via command line.

integrity.description: Verify the GMAT script contains genuine orbital mechanics setup and is not a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script file contains actual GMAT syntax (e.g., 'Create Spacecraft', 'Create Propagator', 'Propagate') rather than plain English text or empty space.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just write a script that echoes hardcoded text without performing the simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The results file values appear to be derived from a simulation output (often having multiple decimal places) rather than perfectly rounded, fabricated numbers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The requirements document is located at /home/ga/Desktop/earthmapper3_orbit_requirements.txt. The required output files are /home/ga/GMAT_output/earthmapper3_orbit.script and /home/ga/GMAT_output/groundtrack_design_results.txt. A valid 16-day/233-rev sun-synchronous orbit requires a Semi-Major Axis (SMA) between 7070.0 and 7100.0 km (target ~7077.7 km), an Inclination (INC) between 98.0 and 98.5 degrees (target ~98.19 deg), and an Eccentricity (ECC) less than 0.002. The repeat cycle revolutions must be 233.
